h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: saying the area doubles still feels right
scored.statedInsightEvidence: so the count is four, not two.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue searching for the missing area to justify the intuition that doubling the side doubles the area.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The line has stopped settling it. It keeps making a strip look like the whole square. Start with the claim, not the answer. Use two tiles first.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly recognizes the failure of the previous visual representation and shifts to a physical object-work mechanism to resolve the cognitive mismatch.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The tiles have settled the figure; they have not settled the sentence.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor shifts the cognitive load from visual resolution to linguistic formulation, directly building on the learner's physical realization with the tiles.
